title: Rotor Dynamics Aeroelastic Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Rotor Dynamics Aeroelastic Inversion: Rotor dynamics identification: infer rotor blade properties from thrust, torque, and vibration measurements. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.blade.element_momentum…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~500; dynamic_stall_effect, tip_loss_factor_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: T = 0.5*rho*A*(Omega*R) ** 2 * C_T(lambda_i, theta, a, e); blade element with flapping equation
E.world_state_x: blade parameters: twist theta_0, collective, flapping a1 b1, stiffness EI
E.observation_y: thrust T (N), torque Q (Nm), vibrational modes from accelerometers

W.regime: Existence of the recovered rotor_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 500); dynamic_stall_effect dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: C_T prediction within 5% for hover conditions. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~500. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |dynamic_stall_effect|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_blade_elements * N_azimuth * N_iter) per operating point per iteration
